title: Jaylen Brown, Jayson Tatum star in blowout Celtics win over Heat: 6 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Jaylen Brown led the Celtics with 43 points in a 147-129 victory over the Miami Heat.
Jayson Tatum recorded a triple-double with 25 points, 18 rebounds, and 11 assists in the Celtics' win.
Sam Hauser contributed 23 points, making all five 3-pointers in the first quarter against the Heat.

title: ​The 2026 Red Sox’ run prevention approach has been a disaster out of the gate
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox begin the 2026 season with a 1-5 record, their worst start since 2019.
Boston's pitching staff, including new additions Suarez, Gray, and Oviedo, has a 5.22 ERA in six games.
The team's defense continues to struggle, committing six errors in their first six games.

title: Stefon Diggs has trial date set in strangulation, assault case
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Stefon Diggs's trial for strangulation and assault is set for May 4 in Dedham District Court.
Diggs faces charges stemming from a December incident with his personal chef over unpaid wages.
The Patriots expressed support for Diggs, who denies the allegations, citing a financial dispute as the cause.

title: ‘What we had talked about, take that to the grave’: Report reveals details about State Police sergeant who allegedly drove drunk, caused fatal crash
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: State Police sergeant Scott Quigley was indicted for negligent motor vehicle homicide while driving under the influence, causing a fatal crash in 2023.
An investigation found State Police did not notify the Middlesex DA's office about the crash, breaching reporting protocols.
Key details about the crash were missing from State Police reports, which state Quigley faced only a civil citation warning.

title: Dorchester woman sentenced for fatal stabbing stemming from online feud
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Alyssa Partsch, 33, sentenced to 15-20 years for fatally stabbing Jazreanna Sheppard in July 2023 after an online feud.
Partsch pleaded guilty to manslaughter, reducing her original second-degree murder charge as part of a plea deal.
Sheppard was attacked at Park Street MBTA station; Partsch previously served time for a similar violent crime.

title: NBA superstar’s message to Connecticut Sun fans: ‘They’ll be alright, they’ve got UConn’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Connecticut Sun WNBA team is relocating to Houston and will be rebranded as the Comets.
Kevin Durant expressed support for the move, joking that Connecticut fans still have UConn's successful women's basketball program.
Former Celtics minority owner Steve Pagliuca had a higher bid to move the Sun to Boston, but the WNBA accepted a record $300 million offer from Houston Rockets owner Tillman Fertitta instead.

title: Alex Cora declines to elaborate after scratching Carlos Narvaez hours before Red Sox game
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Alex Cora removed Carlos Narvaez from the Red Sox lineup before their game against the Astros, sparking speculation.
Cora and Narvaez both declined to elaborate on the reason for the lineup change, insisting it's a team matter.
Connor Wong replaced Narvaez, continuing his strong season start, but the Red Sox lost 6-4 to the Astros.

title: Mike Vrabel says Patriots will pick up Christian Gonzalez’s fifth-year option
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots to exercise Christian Gonzalez's fifth-year option for 2027 season at $18.1 million, Mike Vrabel confirms.
Christian Gonzalez prevented all Seattle receivers from surpassing 61 yards in Super Bowl LX, deflecting three passes.
Gonzalez earned first Pro Bowl selection this past season, despite starting the season with injury setbacks.

title: Elias Lindholm, like the Bruins, is getting hot at the ideal time
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Elias Lindholm has scored five goals and two assists in the last six games for the Boston Bruins.
The Bruins secured their fourth consecutive win with a 6-3 victory against the Dallas Stars.
David Pastrnak recorded three assists, reaching a career-high 66 assists this NHL season.

title: For this Everett runner, the Boston Marathon has always been personal
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Everett resident Michelle Davidson runs the 2026 Boston Marathon representing the Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation.
Davidson is inspired by the 2013 Boston Marathon bombings and aims to support trauma care and honor responders.
The Brigham Stepping Strong Foundation focuses on advancing trauma care and was crucial during the 2013 bombing response.
